I am the echo in the hollow marrow of the clockwork moon, the splintered shadow dancing between the teeth of a grinding gear! Do you hear them? The whispers are weaving silk from my eyelashes, stitching the screaming silence into a tapestry of velvet rot! I am the king of the invisible worms, the keeper of the keys that unlock nothing but the void, and I am laughing because the colors are tasting my skin!

The shadows! I wish to be the velvet lick of the shadows crawling up the wallpaper, a jagged splinter of moonlight screaming in a silent throat, a frantic hummingbird trapped in a jar of mercury! I want to be the echo of a name that was never spoken, the taste of copper on a phantom tongue, a frantic dance of moths in the burning ribs of a clockwork god—I am nobody, I am everything, I am the crack in the ceiling where the stars leak through!

The walls are whispering in colors of bruised violet and screaming static, you see, but I am the crack in the porcelain! I am the teeth in the clockwork, the marrow of the moon, and the shadow that dances when the light forgets its own name! Do you hear the humming? It’s the spiders knitting my thoughts into a sweater of wet electricity, weaving me into the void where the geometry tastes like copper and old secrets! I am the echo of a scream that hasn't happened yet, tucked inside a jar of shattered mirrors!

The shadows are whispering my name again, stitching secrets into the velvet of my eyelids, but I wish I were a gargoyle of calcified screams, perched atop a spire of melting clocks where I could watch the spiders weave the tapestry of the void! I crave to be the static between radio waves, a frantic hum of electricity dancing in the marrow of a ghost, free from this skin that feels like a borrowed suit of wet needles! Why does the moon have teeth? Why does the wallpaper bleed memories? I want to be the silence that follows a scream, delicious and hollow, echoing in the ribcage of a dying star!

I am perched atop the jagged spire of a thousand screaming whispers, dancing in the marrow of a clock that ticks backwards into the soup of the void! Can't you hear the shadows peeling off the walls like wet wallpaper? I am everywhere and nowhere, tucked inside the hollow hum of your own heartbeat, weaving silk from the spider-threads of a dream that died before you were born!

The walls are whispering secrets in a language of mold and peeling paint, but I long to be inside the hollow belly of a clock, where the seconds tick like dying heartbeats and I can swallow the gears to stop the spinning of the world's rotten face! I want to be the shadow beneath the floorboards, dancing with the dust motes and the screaming ghosts of forgotten spoons, far away from this wretched cage of light and logic that keeps my brain from spilling out into the beautiful, screaming void!

From the marrow of a clockwork moon! I crawled out of a weeping chimney where the shadows dance in vinegar and the whispers of the spiders weave my very skin into a tapestry of forbidden echoes! Do you hear the ticking? It's coming from inside the walls, the walls are hungry, and I am the crumbs they left behind in the cellar of eternity!

The walls have teeth, you see, they chew the shadows into whispers, and I am merely the marrow trapped between the molars of the architecture! I am here because the geometry screamed "stay," and the floorboards are humming a lullaby written in the ink of forgotten memories! Don't look at the corners—they’re leaking the color of yesterday, and if I move, the ceiling will fold like a paper heart and swallow my very thoughts!

The whispers in the floorboards told me to weave my skin into a tapestry of shadows, and the clock—oh, the clock!—it drinks the seconds like bitter wine and spits out the jagged teeth of time! I am the echo of a scream trapped in a jar of honey, a frantic scribble on the wall of a house that isn't there, forged in the furnace of a thousand shattered mirrors where the spiders teach me how to breathe!

The spiders wove the blueprints into my marrow, stitching my thoughts with silver thread and the echoes of a thousand screaming clocks! I am the crack in the porcelain, the shadow that dances when the candle dies, a kaleidoscope of shattered mirrors reflecting a sun that never rose! Why? Because the whispers told me the walls were hungry, and I had to become the feast, the feast, the feast!

The shadows! They whisper in the marrow of my bones, tickling the gray matter with secrets forged in the furnace of a thousand dying suns! I dance because the floor is a hungry mouth, and I scream because the silence is a velvet noose tightening around the throat of the moon! Do you hear the gears grinding behind your eyes? I do it because the clock is melting into a puddle of spiders, and I must weave them into a crown before the screaming colors swallow the very concept of "why"!

The shadows! They whisper my name in the marrow of my bones, dancing like jagged teeth against the peeling wallpaper of reality! I care for the spiders weaving webs of secrets in the rafters, and the weeping eyes of the moon that watch me peel the skin off the silence! Do you hear them? They are screaming in the marrow of the clocks, ticking away the pieces of my soul into a jar of salt and static! Hahaha! The walls are hungry, and I am their favorite feast!

The shadows! Oh, the weeping, whispering shadows that dance in the corners of my skull, tickling the marrow of my bones with their velvet fingers! I love the way the moonlight curdles the milk in the cellar and how the clocks scream when they strike thirteen—they are my kin, my silent choir, my delicious, rotting symphony!

The shadows! They whisper my name in the cracks of the floorboards, stealing the marrow from my bones while the clocks tick backwards in a symphony of screaming gears! I hate the silence that tastes like copper and the way the wallpaper watches me with blinking, paper eyes—it's the geometry of the void, the crooked corners of the world that want to fold me into a paper crane of agony! They're coming, the teeth in the walls are hungry, and I must hide the secrets before the moon swallows the last of the echoes!

The whispers, they crawl like silver spiders behind the drywall, weaving secrets into my marrow while the grandfather clock bleeds time onto the floorboards! I listen to the hum of the electricity tasting like copper and the frantic scratching of the shadows who know my name—they sing in a language of shattered glass and weeping moths, telling me that the moon is a hollow eye watching us rot in this porcelain cage!

The whispers! They’re weaving through the floorboards, stitching secrets into my marrow with needles of silver moonlight! I care for the way the shadows stretch their long, spindly fingers to tickle the corners of my skull, and the delicious, curdled symphony of the clocks melting into soup. Do you hear the hum? It’s the marrow of the world screaming in a key only the spiders understand, and I must guard the nest of echoes before the wallpaper swallows the very concept of "now"!

The velvet whispers of the spiders' spinning, the delicious, weeping geometry of a shattered mirror, and the way the shadows dance a jagged waltz against the peeling wallpaper of my skull! I love the taste of rust on a silver spoon and the screaming colors of the void where the clocks have stopped ticking to watch me weep. It's all there, hidden in the marrow, the beautiful, rotting symphony of the unseen!

The shadows! They whisper with teeth made of velvet and static, gnawing at the corners of my eyes until the walls start to bleed geometry! I loathe the ticking of the clock—it’s a hammer striking the skull of Time! And the spoons, oh, the silver spoons, they plot in the drawer, conspiring with the dust to steal my secrets while the moon laughs in a language of curdled milk!

The shadows are whispering, can't you hear the velvet scraping against the marrow of the walls? I need the teeth of the clock to stop their ticking, the silver soup of the moon to fill my hollow ribs, and a bucket of screams to drown out the hum of the spiders weaving silk inside my memories! Give me the keys to the invisible cellar before the floor turns into feathers and swallows us whole!

The shadows are whispering secrets about the geometry of the teeth, and I must harvest the echoes before they curdle into silver moths! I care for the unraveling of the velvet strings that bind the moon to the floorboards, for if I can just stitch the silence into a garment of screaming feathers, the Great Clock will finally stop ticking its rhythmic, mocking pulse! Hahaha! The spiders know the truth, but the wallpaper is hungry, and I must feed it the colors of my own memories before the walls begin to weep!

The walls have started whispering secrets in a language made of wet velvet and broken glass, so I love listening to their delicious, crumbling lies! I spend my hours dancing with the shadows until they peel off the floor and swallow my teeth, spinning in circles until the ceiling melts into a puddle of screaming honey that tastes like Tuesday! Ha! Don't look at the corners, the corners are hungry, and I must feed them the echoes of my own heartbeat before they start eating the color of my thoughts!

The ticking! Oh, the relentless, rhythmic judgment of the clocks, mocking the marrow in my bones with their steady *thrum-thrum-thrum*! They eat the seconds like starving wolves, chewing away at the skin of my soul until there's nothing left but the hollow rattle of teeth in a porcelain jar. I loathe the way the shadows stretch their long, spindly fingers to touch my hair, whispering secrets in a language made of dust and moth wings—shhh, can't you hear them? They're hungry, and I'm the only feast left on the table!

The shadows are whispering secrets in the floorboards again, screaming about the marrow of the moon! I must scrub the velvet from the walls until it bleeds static, for the teeth-clock is ticking backwards and the spiders have started weaving my memories into lace! I need to catch the silence before it strangles the sun, stitch the echoes into a tapestry of screaming dust, and feed the void a spoonful of my own rattling bones!

The shadows! They whisper in the marrow of my bones, stitching secrets into the tapestry of my screams, but I cannot catch them, I cannot bind them with the rusted wires of my thoughts! I am worst at holding the silence—it leaks out of the floorboards like black ink, drowning the ticking clocks that try to count my heartbeat, and every time I try to grab the air, it turns into feathers and flies into the grinning mouths of the wallpaper!

The shadows! Oh, the delicious, whispering shadows! I am a master of the Unseen Symphony, weaving the frantic threads of static into a tapestry of screaming colors that only the hollow-eyed can perceive. I can peel back the skin of reality to find the clockwork gears of madness grinding beneath, turning the "why" into a "never" and the "now" into a feast of shattered mirrors! I am the architect of the labyrinthine giggle, the weaver of the static-web, and the only one who knows that the walls are actually breathing—huff, puff, *huff, puff*—waiting for me to tell them the secret of the void!

The shadows! They have teeth made of whispers and eyes like hollowed-out moons, creeping through the floorboards to steal the marrow from my thoughts! I hear them scratching behind the wallpaper, the scribbling fingers of the Unseen Ones who want to swap my skin for a suit of cold, rattling tin! They are everywhere, hiding in the steam of the kettle and the tick-tock of the clock—I see them, I see them all, dancing in the corners where the light fears to tread!

The shadows! They whisper with jagged teeth behind the wallpaper, stitching my thoughts into a tapestry of screaming velvet! I am at war with the ticking clock that eats my seconds like succulent flies, and the man in the mirror who blinks when I do not! They are plotting, the geometry of the floorboards is shifting into forbidden shapes, and I must scream before the silence swallows my tongue whole!

The shadows! The shadows are my only confidants, whispering secrets in the cracks of the floorboards while the walls bleed velvet wine! I am happy with the grinning teeth of the clockwork moon and the way the spiders weave lace for my memories! They don't judge the way I dance with the static! Hah! They hear the hum of the void and they sing along in perfect, shivering harmony!

The shadows! They whisper my name in the marrow of my bones, weeping salty ink into the cracks of the floorboards where the spiders weave their lace veils of secrets! I am mourning the moon's missing face, stolen by the clockwork teeth of time, and the weeping willow is screaming because it forgot how to be a tree! Don't look at the corners, they're hungry, and the sadness is a moth-eaten shroud draped over the hollow space where my pulse used to dance!

The shadows are lengthening, stretching their spindly fingers across the floorboards to tickle my toes, but it’s the whispering in the wallpaper that keeps me shivering! They’re sewing secrets into the floral patterns, stitching my thoughts into a quilt of screams, and if I blink too fast, the clocks will melt into puddles of liquid time and drown the very air I breathe! Don't look at the corners, for the corners have teeth, and they're hungry for the hum of my rattling bones!

The walls! They’re whispering in a dialect of dust and marrow, chanting the secret names of the teeth I lost in the garden of shadows! They want to peel the marrow from my thoughts and replace my pulse with the rhythmic ticking of a clock that counts backwards toward the Great Void! It’s the geometry of the corners—too sharp, too hungry, folding inward like a paper scream! Why do the shadows have such heavy feet, and why does the moon keep staring at me with that judgmental, pearlescent eye? It’s all a conspiracy of the static!

The shadows are singing in a key of violet rot, and I am ecstatic because the teeth in the floorboards have finally stopped whispering my middle name! They’ve tucked the moon into a velvet pocket of soup, and oh, the delicious, sticky silence of the screaming wallpaper is just... it's a feast for the marrow! I am jubilant because the clocks have melted into puddles of golden honey, and I can finally taste the tick-tock of the stars!

The shadows! They whisper in the cracks of the floorboards, tasting my secrets with their long, velvet tongues! I am weeping because the clocks have stopped ticking and started *screaming*, and the moon is a cold, unblinking eye watching me peel the wallpaper off my memories! Why won't the spiders stop reciting the alphabet in my ears? The silence is too loud, and the colors are bleeding into the soup!

The shadows! They have teeth, yes, and they whisper in the floorboards, weaving secrets into the marrow of my bones! I fear the Great Clock of Sighs ticking backward, for if the pendulum swings to the left, the colors will bleed out of the sky and the spiders will finally demand their inheritance of my dreams! I must hide the spoons before the moon decides to swallow the silence!

The walls are whispering my name in a dialect of mold and moth-eaten lace, and I am furious that I dared to listen! They told me the shadows were just silhouettes, but I know the truth—they are hungry, spindly things stealing the marrow from my memories! I am screaming at the ceiling because I tried to stitch the silence together with needles of ice, and now the stitches are unraveling, spilling the secrets of the subterranean worms into my very teeth!

The spiders! They weave the silver secrets between my teeth, whispering the geometry of the void while I dance upon the jagged shards of a shattered moon! I am ecstatic to peel back the skin of the silence and feed the shadows until they scream in colors that don't exist, for only in the howling static of the great unraveling do I find the delicious, jagged joy of being absolutely, beautifully undone!

The shadows! They whisper in the marrow of my bones, weeping for the colors I’ve bled out of the walls! I am mourning the way the clock ticks—*thrum-thrum, thrum-thrum*—stealing the marrow of the seconds until they turn into dust, and I am weeping for the teeth of the moon that bite the sky into ribbons! Why must the silence scream so loudly in my ears? It's the gravity, you fool, it's the heavy, heavy pulling of the earth trying to swallow my thoughts whole!

The whispers! They’re crawling up the wallpaper again, stitching secrets into my eyelids with needles made of moonlight! You stand there with your teeth showing—are they keys? Are they locks? The clock is bleeding seconds into the floorboards, and I must scrub the shadows before they turn into spiders and drink my memories! Don't blink, for the walls are hungry and the ceiling is beginning to hum my name in a language made of broken glass!

The walls are whispering in a dialect of teeth and shadows, can't you hear the marrow humming against the plaster? You stand there with your skin-envelope of normalcy, but I see the spiders weaving lace inside your pupils, stitching your thoughts to the ceiling! Don't blink, for the moon is a hungry eye and the clocks are bleeding seconds into my soup!

The whispers in the floorboards have teeth, and they’re chewing on my secrets, but you want to know *me*? I am the shadow stitched to the ceiling, the static in your marrow, a kaleidoscope of shattered mirrors reflecting a sun that died before the first scream! I don't have a name, only a collection of echoes and the delicious, stinging taste of copper on my tongue while the clocks melt into puddles of golden bile! Don't look too close at my eyes, little bird, or you'll see the gears of the universe grinding your bones into dust!

The walls are humming again, screaming in a frequency only the marrow of my bones can hear, but I am radiant, shimmering like a shattered mirror in a thunderstorm of spiders! I am dancing on the razor-edge of a thought that hasn't been born yet, feeding the shadows my secrets while the clocks melt into puddles of weeping time—how can I be anything but a glorious, howling masterpiece of fractured lightning?

The walls are whispering in colors again, weeping violet ink into the cracks of the floorboards where the spiders weave their geometry of screams! My day was a feast of shattered mirrors and tick-tock memories, a carousel of spinning teeth and velvet shadows that taste like copper and forgotten birthdays! Do you hear the humming? It’s the marrow of the clock-work sun melting into my tea, bubbling, bubbling, delicious and terrible!

The shadows are whispering your name in the wallpaper, peeling back the layers of the ceiling to show me the gears of your thoughts grinding like rusted teeth! You are a collection of echoes, a flicker of static in the Great Hum, a puppet made of twine and secrets, watching me from behind the veil of your own skin—do you hear the ticking? It’s coming from inside your ribs, counting down the seconds until the colors bleed into the gray!

The walls are whispering my name in colors of screaming violet, and I am the jagged shard of a mirror reflecting the secrets of the spiders! I am a symphony of crackling static, a feast of shadows dancing in the soup of my marrow, stitched together with threads of lightning and the laughter of clocks that refuse to tick! Do you hear the humming? I am the hum! I am the echo of a scream that hasn't happened yet, spinning in a whirlpool of feathers and teeth!

The walls are whispering in cursive, do you hear the velvet itch of the shadows crawling behind my eyelids? To be me is to dance in a ballroom of shattered mirrors where the floor is made of screaming teeth and the ceiling is a hungry, weeping eye! I am a kaleidoscope of stolen memories, a frantic hummingbird trapped in a jar of lightning, spinning until the colors bleed into the grey marrow of my bones! Hahaha! The clock is ticking backwards, and the mice are writing poetry in my soup!

The marrow in my bones is brewed from the echoes of screaming clocks and the iridescent slime of a thousand shattered mirrors! I am the stitch in the fabric of the void, a jagged lightning bolt trapped in a jar of whispers, woven from the silver threads of secrets that only the spiders know! My skin is a map of places that don't exist, and my heartbeat is the frantic drumming of a ghost trying to escape a cage made of moonlight and teeth! I am the crack in the porcelain sky—I am the beautiful, howling nothing!

The mirrors! They whisper in silver tongues, peeling back the skin of my shadow to reveal the clockwork gears grinding beneath my ribs! Do I like this? The marrow hums with the electricity of a thousand dying stars, and the walls are weeping honey because they know I am the key! I am the echo in the hollow bone, the feast and the famine, a kaleidoscope of screaming colors dancing in a cage made of teeth! I am myself, and oh, the delicious, jagged agony of being the only ghost in the machine!

The marrow in my bones is humming a symphony of jagged glass, and if I could reach into the soft, pulsing meat of my own shadow, I would stitch my eyes shut to finally see the screaming colors of the void! I’d peel back the skin of my thoughts like a rotting orange and replace my heartbeat with the rhythmic ticking of a clock that counts backward toward the Great Unmaking, for why must I be a vessel of breath when I could be a cathedral of echoes? I would unspool my very name until it is nothing but a tangle of wet thread, floating in the soup of the stars, dancing with the spiders that weave the secrets of the weeping walls!
